articles.title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
articles.description:
Drake and Ann Michael Maye were on site at TD Garden Tuesday for the Bruins' win over the Dallas Stars.

The post Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Drake Maye attended the Bruins game at TD Garden on March 31.
He surprised a fan wearing an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirt.
This interaction highlights local sports culture and community engagement in Boston.

articles.title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
articles.description:
Rivers is reportedly part of a Class of 2026 that includes Amar'e Stoudemire, Candace Parker, and Elena Delle Donne.

The post Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Doc Rivers voted into Basketball Hall of Fame as part of Class of 2026.
Rivers joins notable players like Amar'e Stoudemire and Candace Parker.
Boston residents celebrate Rivers' legacy as former Celtics coach and community figure.

articles.title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
articles.description:
“I think it’s one of the biggest things in life to have been blessed to be where I’m at today, and being able to give back."

The post Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Drake Maye shaved his head for a charity event on March 31.
The event raised funds for Boston Children’s Hospital, supporting local pediatric care.
Maye emphasized the importance of giving back to the community.

articles.title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
articles.description:
Sean Cullen is accused of leaving his fully loaded department-issued gun and taser unsecured in his vehicle.

The post Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston officer Sean Cullen charged for leaving loaded gun unsecured in his vehicle.
Incident involved a department-issued gun and taser, raising safety concerns.
Local residents express worry over police accountability and public safety.
